title: Checkmark Draw + Glow Animation Polish
deliverable: The circular checkbox renders an SVG checkmark that draws itself via stroke-dashoffset (~300ms ease-out) on completion, and a gold glow ring pulses via box-shadow (~500ms ease-out). Both animations sequence within the ~500ms pre-state-update window established by Block 4, so the user feels the reward before the card exits.

in: SVG checkmark path inside the circular checkbox, precise d= attribute
stroke-dasharray and stroke-dashoffset values for the checkmark draw animation
CSS @keyframes or inline style transition for gold glow ring box-shadow pulse (~500ms ease-out)
Sequencing: checkmark starts at t=0ms, glow starts at t=0ms, both complete within ~500ms, then Block 4 card exit fires
Checked visual state (filled circle background, white/gold checkmark stroke) vs. unchecked state (hollow circle, transparent stroke)
Checkbox size: 28px visible circle with ≥44px tap target via padding (per PRD §5.3)
Accessibility: aria-label on the checkbox button, role=checkbox, aria-checked state
Gold accent colour from CSS var for the glow (--color-gold)
Reduced-motion fallback: instant check with no animation if prefers-reduced-motion

files.purpose: Self-contained circular checkbox component that owns the SVG checkmark and glow animation; accepts checked, onComplete, disabled props
CSS @keyframes for checkmark-draw and glow-pulse; also defines .checkbox--checked, .checkbox--animating classes and reduced-motion overrides
Replace inline checkbox element with <CheckboxButton> and wire the onComplete callback so Block 4 completion flow triggers after the animation window

implementation_steps.description: Define the CSS custom properties for the checkbox
implementation_steps.details: In the project's global CSS (or as inline vars on :root), ensure --color-gold (#D4AF37 or nearest brand value), --color-emerald-dark (background), --checkbox-size: 28px, --checkbox-tap: 44px. These vars are consumed by CheckboxButton and checkmark.css. If a global CSS file already exists from Block 1, add vars there rather than creating a new one.

The checkmark sits inside a 20×20 viewBox (the inner drawing area of a 28px circle). The path is a two-segment polyline: M 4 10 L 8.5 15 L 16 6. This gives a classic tick: left foot at (4,10), vertex at (8.5,15), right tip at (16,6). Use stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round'. The total arc length of this path is approximately 19.5px (measured: segment 1 = √((8.5-4)²+(15-10)²) ≈ 6.73, segment 2 = √((16-8.5)²+(6-15)²) ≈ 11.72, total ≈ 18.45 — round up to 19 for dasharray). Set stroke-dasharray='19' stroke-dashoffset='19' when unchecked; animate to stroke-dashoffset='0' when checked.

Define: @keyframes checkmark-draw { from { stroke-dashoffset: 19; } to { stroke-dashoffset: 0; } } — duration 300ms, timing ease-out. Define: @keyframes glow-pulse { 0% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 0 rgba(212, 175, 55, 0); } 30% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 6px rgba(212, 175, 55, 0.55), 0 0 12px 4px rgba(212, 175, 55, 0.30); } 100% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(212, 175, 55, 0.15), 0 0 6px 2px rgba(212, 175, 55, 0.10); } } — duration 500ms, timing ease-out, fill-mode forwards. Define @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) overrides: set animation-duration to 0.01ms for both keyframes so the check appears instantly.

Props: { checked: bool, onComplete: () => void, disabled: bool }. Internal state: animating (bool). On click when not checked and not disabled: (1) set animating=true immediately to trigger CSS classes, (2) call onComplete() — Block 4's handler already delays state update by ~500ms so the animation plays out. When checked prop becomes true (from Block 4 state), the component stays in checked+animating state for the animation duration; after the animation Block 4 unmounts the card anyway. The button element has: role='checkbox', aria-checked={checked}, aria-label='Görevi tamamla'. Inline style: width 44px, height 44px, display flex, alignItems center, justifyContent center, background transparent, border none, cursor pointer, padding 8px, borderRadius '50%'. The 28px circle div inside has: width 28px, height 28px, borderRadius '50%', border '2px solid var(--color-gold)' unchecked / background var(--color-gold) checked, transition 'background 150ms ease'. Apply class 'checkbox--animating' when animating===true to trigger glow-pulse on the circle div.

Inside the 28px circle div, render: <svg width='20' height='20' viewBox='0 0 20 20' fill='none' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' style={{ position:'absolute', top:'50%', left:'50%', transform:'translate(-50%,-50%)' }}><path d='M 4 10 L 8.5 15 L 16 6' stroke='white' strokeWidth='2' strokeLinecap='round' strokeLinejoin='round' style={{ strokeDasharray: 19, strokeDashoffset: checked ? 0 : 19, animation: animating ? 'checkmark-draw 300ms ease-out forwards' : 'none' }} /></svg>. When not checked and not animating, dashoffset=19 keeps the path invisible. The animation class draws it. After Block 4 unmounts the card there is no need to reset.

Block 4's completion handler does: (a) call triggerAnimations() — which calls onComplete on the checkbox — then (b) waits ~500ms, then (c) runs state update + card exit. CheckboxButton fires at t=0ms: checkmark draw starts (300ms), glow pulse starts (500ms). By t=300ms checkmark is fully drawn. By t=500ms glow has settled to dim ring. At t=500ms Block 4 begins card exit (translateX + opacity + scale). The entire reward is visible before dismissal. Document this contract in a JSDoc comment on CheckboxButton: '@param onComplete — called immediately on tap; caller (Block 4) must delay state mutation by ≥500ms to allow animations to complete'.

Import CheckboxButton. In the task card render, replace the existing checkbox button (created in Block 4 as a plain button/div) with <CheckboxButton checked={false} onComplete={() => handleComplete(task.id)} disabled={isExiting} />. The isExiting flag (from Block 4's exit animation state) prevents double-taps. Confirm that the checked prop is never set to true externally — the card exits before that matters, so the prop is always false while the card is mounted; animating internal state handles the visual.

In checkmark.css under @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce): .checkbox--animating path { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; } and .checkbox--animating .checkbox-circle { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; }. This makes the check appear instantly for users who have configured prefers-reduced-motion. The 500ms Block 4 delay still applies (it is a setTimeout, not an animation), so the card still exits after ~500ms even in reduced-motion mode — which is acceptable as it gives a brief moment for the user to register the check.

data_structures.name: CheckboxButtonProps
data_structures.shape: { checked: boolean; onComplete: () => void; disabled?: boolean }
data_structures.notes: checked is always false while card is mounted (card unmounts on exit); onComplete is called immediately on tap so Block 4 can start its 500ms delay; disabled prevents double-tap during exit animation

data_structures.name: CheckboxButtonState
data_structures.notes: Set to true on tap; drives CSS class checkbox--animating; never reset because card unmounts after animation window

data_structures.name: CheckmarkPath
data_structures.shape: { d: 'M 4 10 L 8.5 15 L 16 6'; viewBox: '0 0 20 20'; strokeDasharray: 19; strokeDashoffset_unchecked: 19; strokeDashoffset_checked: 0 }
data_structures.notes: Total path length ≈18.45px, rounded to 19 for dasharray. Measured: leg1=√(4.5²+5²)≈6.73, leg2=√(7.5²+9²)≈11.72, sum=18.45

data_structures.name: GlowKeyframe
data_structures.notes: Peak glow at 30% (150ms into 500ms) for snappy feel; settles to dim ring rather than cutting to nothing — avoids jarring disappearance before card exits

interfaces.name: CheckboxButton
interfaces.signature: CheckboxButton({ checked: boolean, onComplete: () => void, disabled?: boolean }): JSX.Element
interfaces.purpose: Self-contained circular checkbox with SVG checkmark draw and glow pulse animation; calls onComplete immediately on tap; Block 4 owns the 500ms delay before state mutation

interfaces.name: handleComplete (Queue.jsx)
interfaces.purpose: Block 4's completion handler; must accept being called from CheckboxButton's onComplete; must NOT trigger state update until ~500ms later (already the case from Block 4)

acceptance_criteria: Tapping the circular checkbox immediately starts the SVG checkmark drawing itself from top-left of the tick to bottom-right, completing in ~300ms with ease-out timing
Simultaneously with the checkmark draw, a gold glow ring appears around the circle, peaks at ~150ms (30% of 500ms), and settles to a dim ring by 500ms
The checkmark is fully drawn and glow has peaked before the card begins its exit animation (checkmark done at 300ms, card exit starts at ~500ms)
The circle fills with gold/emerald background as the checkmark draws (checked visual state)
The checkmark uses white stroke on a filled circle background for contrast
The button has role='checkbox', aria-checked, and aria-label='Görevi tamamla'
Double-tap during the exit animation is prevented (disabled prop from Block 4's isExiting state)
With prefers-reduced-motion: the checkmark appears instantly, glow appears instantly, the 500ms delay still applies before card exits
No layout shift occurs: the checkbox container dimensions are fixed regardless of animation state
The animation feels tactile — the reward is perceptible as a distinct moment before the card leaves
verification: Tap the top task's checkbox — visually confirm checkmark draws itself (not a flash) over ~300ms
Visually confirm a gold glow ring pulses around the circle simultaneously
Confirm card exit does NOT begin until after the checkmark is fully drawn (~300ms delay between full draw and exit start is acceptable but exit must not overlap checkmark draw noticeably)
Open DevTools → Elements: confirm SVG path stroke-dashoffset transitions from 19 to 0
Open DevTools → Elements: confirm box-shadow on circle element animates during completion
Enable prefers-reduced-motion in OS or DevTools → Rendering → confirm check appears instantly with no animation
Use DevTools accessibility inspector: confirm role=checkbox, aria-checked=false on unchecked checkbox
Tap rapidly twice — confirm second tap is ignored (no double-animation, no double-complete)
Measure animation with DevTools Performance tab: checkmark-draw 300ms, glow-pulse 500ms, card exit begins after glow-pulse
Verify on a mobile viewport (375px width): checkbox tap target ≥44px, no overflow, circle centred in card

questionable_decisions.decision: SVG path total length hardcoded to 19 (rounded up from 18.45) rather than measured at runtime via getTotalLength()
questionable_decisions.assumption: The path M 4 10 L 8.5 15 L 16 6 has a fixed, analytically calculable length that does not vary across browsers. Rounding to 19 leaves 0.55px of invisible overdraw which is imperceptible.
alternatives: Use a useEffect + pathRef.current.getTotalLength() to measure at mount and set dasharray/dashoffset dynamically — guarantees pixel-perfect across any path change
Use a longer dasharray (e.g. 30) as a safe overestimate — ensures full draw even if length estimate is off
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Keep hardcoded 19 for simplicity; the path is static and the rounding error is invisible. Add a comment with the calculation. If the path ever changes, re-measure.

questionable_decisions.decision: Glow is applied as box-shadow on a div, not as an SVG filter (feGaussianBlur drop-shadow) or outline
questionable_decisions.assumption: box-shadow on a border-radius: 50% div produces a circular glow ring that matches the visual intent. SVG filters would require additional SVG wrapper complexity and are harder to animate with CSS keyframes alone.
alternatives: SVG feDropShadow filter on the circle element — more visually accurate circular glow but requires SVG markup for the circle
CSS filter: drop-shadow() on the outer div — simpler but drop-shadow does not support the layered spread/blur combination
outline + box-shadow combo for a hard ring + soft halo
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Keep box-shadow. It produces a good enough glow ring, is easily animated, and keeps the component simple. The layered two-value box-shadow (spread ring + blur halo) gives depth.

questionable_decisions.decision: animating state is never reset to false inside CheckboxButton (the card unmounts before reset would matter)
questionable_decisions.assumption: Block 4 always unmounts the task card within ~500ms of onComplete being called. If for any reason Block 4 does not unmount (e.g. error in state update), the checkbox stays in glow state indefinitely with the animation frozen at fill-mode: forwards.
alternatives: Reset animating to false after 600ms via setTimeout inside CheckboxButton — makes the component self-contained and robust to unmount failures
Use a CSS animation-fill-mode: none and reset dashoffset via state — cleaner but requires tracking checked externally
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Add a 600ms setTimeout to reset animating=false as a defensive measure. This also enables re-tap if Block 4 fails, though onComplete should be idempotent. Low cost, higher robustness.

questionable_decisions.decision: Checkmark draw animation starts at t=0ms simultaneous with glow pulse, rather than sequencing checkmark first then glow
questionable_decisions.assumption: Simultaneous start feels more immediate and 'snappy' for ADHD users who benefit from instant feedback. A sequential stagger (e.g. glow starts at 150ms) would feel slower to initiate.
alternatives: Glow starts at 0ms, checkmark draw starts at 50ms delay — glow rings in slightly ahead, checkmark follows
Checkmark draws 0–300ms, glow pulses 100–600ms — slight stagger keeps total window under 600ms but extends past 500ms Block 4 boundary
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Keep simultaneous t=0 start. Both animations complete within the 500ms window (checkmark at 300ms, glow at 500ms). Simultaneous onset feels most responsive. If glow were delayed to 100ms, peak would hit at 250ms (30% of 500ms from 100ms start = 250ms), which is within window.

open_questions: What is the exact gold hex value (#D4AF37 assumed)? If the design uses a different --color-gold value, the glow rgba values in keyframes should be updated to match.

Does Block 4's 500ms delay timer start from the tap event or from some other trigger? If it starts from a different point (e.g. after sound synthesis in Block 5), the sequencing assumption may need adjustment.
Should the circle border colour animate from emerald/white to gold during the draw, or stay gold throughout? PRD does not specify the unchecked border colour explicitly.
Is the checkmark stroke colour white (#FFFFFF) or a specific emerald-contrast colour? On a gold-filled circle, white has high contrast but a deep emerald stroke might fit the theme better.

Should the checkbox retain its checked+glow state briefly after the 500ms mark (while Block 4's card exit is in progress), or should the glow fade before the card begins moving? Currently the glow settles to a dim ring by 500ms which overlaps with the card exit — is this visually acceptable?
